consistency.class_match: The feature correctly lists the class as 'paladin'.
consistency.level_match: The feature is listed at level 14, which is consistent with known D&D 5e class progression for this specific ability.
consistency.mechanic_consistency: The description aligns with the intended effect of a high-level Paladin feature focused on spell removal, similar to the existing 'Aura of Purity' mechanic but focused on self/touch.

quality.clarity: The description is clear regarding action economy ('use your action'), target ('yourself or on one willing creature that you touch'), and effect ('end one spell').
quality.completeness: All necessary mechanical components (trigger, action, target, effect, usage limit, and reset condition) are present.
quality.wording_adherence: The language used is standard for official rule texts.

mechanics.resource_management: Uses equal to Charisma modifier (min 1), refreshed on a long rest.
mechanics.effect_type: Spell termination/dispelling on a touch target.
mechanics.power_level_assessment: At level 14, having an action-based, limited-use spell removal ability usable on allies is powerful, especially since it bypasses needing a save or a dispel check (it auto-succeeds for the target/self). This is a significant defensive tool appropriate for high-tier play.

suggestions: No mechanical changes are suggested as the data appears to represent a complete and standard ability.

Verify against official source material if this feature exists, as it strongly resembles features found in certain Paladin subclasses or optional class features, rather than a core Paladin class feature at this level (which typically grants Aura of Purity).
